content.text:
{"id":"8ee40cbb-a2e5-43b1-b8a8-6c94ee3b9b3b","identifier":"FRG-206","title":"Bug: Characters never talk to each other","description":"AIEngine.\_applyDecision only handles move/interact/idle — there is no talk action. Characters never approach each other for conversation.

## What exists

* AIEngine.\_requestDecision builds a prompt with nearby objects, but NOT nearby characters
* The action choices in the prompt are: move, interact, idle
* Character has: currentDialogue, dialogueTimer, pendingSpeech properties
* SimulationEngine already handles pendingSpeech in its update loop

## Implementation

1. In AIEngine.\_requestDecision: add nearby characters to the prompt context (name, position, current action)
2. Add 'talk' as a fourth action choice in the user prompt:
   { "action": "talk", "targetCharacter": "character_name", "dialogue": "...", "description": "..." }
3. In AIEngine.\_applyDecision: add 'talk' case
   * Find target character by name
   * Walk Character A to adjacent tile of Character B (use findAdjacentWalkable)
   * Set Character A's dialogue
   * Set Character B's pendingSpeech to a contextual response (can be from the AI response or a simple acknowledgment)
4. Add conversation events to SimulationEngine.pendingEvents so the debug event log captures them

## Dependencies

* Depends on FRG-218 (AIEngine wired into debug page) to test on debug page
* Can be developed against main page first if needed

## Acceptance

* AI prompt includes nearby characters as context
* Characters occasionally choose 'talk' action targeting another character
* Character A walks to Character B and speaks
* Character B responds with dialogue
* Conversation appears in debug event log with both participants","projectMilestone":{"id":"d98e487a-66f2-4106-86d3-550aac4cb898","name":"M1: Sim engine correct (debug foundation)"},"priority":{"value":1,"name":"Urgent"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-206/bug-characters-never-talk-to-each-other","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-206-bug-characters-never-talk-to-each-other","createdAt":"2026-03-07T01:01:09.188Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-07T04:04:36.641Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Todo","labels":[],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"AI Aquarium","projectId":"0385a8c7-ba5a-43a3-b223-acf4d1ad6d63","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"}

content.text:
Perfect! I've successfully found and read all three files. Here's a summary of what I found:

## Files Located and Read

### 1. AIEngine.js
**Path:** `/home/gem/project/src/AIEngine.js`

This is an LLM-driven decision engine that enables AI-powered character behavior. Key features:
- Can be disabled (default) or enabled with an API endpoint and optional API key
- Polls each character independently on a configurable interval (default 30 seconds)
- Compatible with both Anthropic and OpenAI-style API responses
- Sends system and user prompts to the LLM with character context (mood, energy, nearby objects)
- Supports three actions: move, interact, and idle
- Handles dialogue and description output from the LLM
- Prevents overlapping requests and skips decisions while character is interacting
- Applies AI decisions by setting movement targets, object interactions, or idle states

### 2. Engine.js (SimulationEngine)
**Path:** `/home/gem/project/src/Engine.js`

This is the core simulation engine managing characters, objects, and default behavior. Key features:
- Manages a grid-based world with walkable/non-walkable tiles
- Optionally integrates with AIEngine for LLM decisions
- Includes an ObjectEngine for state machine-driven objects (Japanese home objects)
- Implements a sim clock that runs at 60x speed (1 real second = 60 sim seconds)
- Handles random wander behavior with 30% chance of object interaction
- Manages character-object interactions with hold durations
- Has speech/dialogue system with random speech bubbles
- Provides helper methods for pathfinding and adjacent tile checks
- Queues events (emoji floats) for the scene to consume

### 3. Character.js
**Path:** `/home/gem/project/src/Character.js`

This is a tile-based character class with pathfinding and state management. Key features:
- Pure JavaScript implementation (no Phaser core dependency, though can integrate with Phaser sprites)
- Implements A* pathfinding algorithm for grid-based movement
- Three action states: idle, walking, interacting
- Supports sprite animation with directional facing (up, down, left, right)
- Personality stats: mood and energy (0-100)
- Handles dialogue display with timers
- Preset dialogue lines for random speech bubbles
- Object interaction targeting system
- Pixel position computed from tile position with smooth movement interpolation
- Perception system to detect nearby objects within radius

All three files are well-documented with comments explaining their purpose and functionality.

content.text:
Perfect! I've successfully found and read all three files. Here's a summary of what I found:

## Files Located and Read

### 1. AIEngine.js
**Path:** `/home/gem/project/src/AIEngine.js`

This is an LLM-driven decision engine that enables AI-powered character behavior. Key features:
- Can be disabled (default) or enabled with an API endpoint and optional API key
- Polls each character independently on a configurable interval (default 30 seconds)
- Compatible with both Anthropic and OpenAI-style API responses
- Sends system and user prompts to the LLM with character context (mood, energy, nearby objects)
- Supports three actions: move, interact, and idle
- Handles dialogue and description output from the LLM
- Prevents overlapping requests and skips decisions while character is interacting
- Applies AI decisions by setting movement targets, object interactions, or idle states

### 2. Engine.js (SimulationEngine)
**Path:** `/home/gem/project/src/Engine.js`

This is the core simulation engine managing characters, objects, and default behavior. Key features:
- Manages a grid-based world with walkable/non-walkable tiles
- Optionally integrates with AIEngine for LLM decisions
- Includes an ObjectEngine for state machine-driven objects (Japanese home objects)
- Implements a sim clock that runs at 60x speed (1 real second = 60 sim seconds)
- Handles random wander behavior with 30% chance of object interaction
- Manages character-object interactions with hold durations
- Has speech/dialogue system with random speech bubbles
- Provides helper methods for pathfinding and adjacent tile checks
- Queues events (emoji floats) for the scene to consume

### 3. Character.js
**Path:** `/home/gem/project/src/Character.js`

This is a tile-based character class with pathfinding and state management. Key features:
- Pure JavaScript implementation (no Phaser core dependency, though can integrate with Phaser sprites)
- Implements A* pathfinding algorithm for grid-based movement
- Three action states: idle, walking, interacting
- Supports sprite animation with directional facing (up, down, left, right)
- Personality stats: mood and energy (0-100)
- Handles dialogue display with timers
- Preset dialogue lines for random speech bubbles
- Object interaction targeting system
- Pixel position computed from tile position with smooth movement interpolation
- Perception system to detect nearby objects within radius

All three files are well-documented with comments explaining their purpose and functionality.

content.thinking: Let me read the actual source files to understand the implementation details.

Now I have a good understanding of the codebase. Let me plan the implementation:

1. **In `AIEngine._requestDecision`**: 
   - The function currently receives `characters` from `update()`, but `_requestDecision` only receives `character, objects, simState`. I need to also pass `characters` to `_requestDecision` so I can include nearby characters in the prompt.
   - Actually looking at the `update` method - it has `characters` available. I need to pass the characters list to `_requestDecision`.

2. **Add 'talk' action to prompt** - Add the fourth action choice.
   
3. **In `AIEngine._applyDecision`**: I'll handle the 'talk' case by locating the target character by name, moving Character A to an adjacent tile next to Character B using the existing pathfinding utility, setting up Character A's dialogue, and then populating Character B's `pendingSpeech` with a contextual response.

4. **Add conversation events to `SimulationEngine.pendingEvents`** - Since `simState` is already passed to `_applyDecision` and contains access to `findAdjacentWalkable`, I can use that same object to push conversation events into the pending events queue.

Now I'm tracing the flow: `update()` calls `_requestDecision()` which receives `simState`, but I also need to pass `characters` through so the decision logic can reference other characters. I'll add a `pushEvent` callback to the `simState` object alongside the existing utilities, then update the `AIEngine` methods to accept and use both `characters` and the enhanced `simState`.

Implementing the changes... I'm settling on passing `characters` as a parameter through both `_requestDecision` and `_applyDecision` since that keeps the data flow explicit. Now I need to update the `Engine.js` call to pass the characters array, and add `pushEvent` to the `simState` object so the AI engine can emit conversation events.

Implementing the talk action... I need to add a `targetDialogue` field to the AI prompt so it can generate both sides of the conversation—what Character A says and what Character B should respond with. Then I'll update the logic to set Character B's `pendingSpeech` from that response field, and I'm also thinking about how to include context about nearby characters when requesting the decision.

Updating the action format...

Now I need to think about how conversation events should be structured in the pending events array. Looking at the existing event shapes in the engine, they have properties like emoji and speech. For conversations to show up properly in the debug log with both participants, I should create a new event type that captures the speaker, target, and their dialogue exchange.

For the `_applyDecision` method, I'm working through the dialogue handling in the 'talk' case. Character A's speech gets set via the existing `currentDialogue` assignment, and I need to make sure Character B receives their response through `pendingSpeech`. The current code structure should handle this correctly without double-setting anything.

Writing the talk case implementation... Still writing the implementation...

input.old_string:
/**
 * AIEngine.js — LLM-driven decision engine.
 *
 * When `endpoint` is null (default) the engine is disabled and characters
 * fall back to random wander controlled by SimulationEngine.
 *
 * To enable, pass an endpoint URL and optionally an API key:
 *   new AIEngine({ endpoint: 'https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages', apiKey: '...' })
 *
 * The engine polls each character independently on a configurable interval.
 * Responses are expected as JSON with this shape:
 *   { "action": "move|interact|idle",
 *     "targetId": "object_id",          // for interact
 *     "targetTile": { "tx": 5, "ty": 9 }, // for move
 *     "dialogue": "optional speech",
 *     "description": "what the character is doing" }
 */
input.new_string:
/**
 * AIEngine.js — LLM-driven decision engine.
 *
 * When `endpoint` is null (default) the engine is disabled and characters
 * fall back to random wander controlled by SimulationEngine.
 *
 * To enable, pass an endpoint URL and optionally an API key:
 *   new AIEngine({ endpoint: 'https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages', apiKey: '...' })
 *
 * The engine polls each character independently on a configurable interval.
 * Responses are expected as JSON with this shape:
 *   { "action": "move|interact|idle|talk",
 *     "targetId": "object_id",               // for interact
 *     "targetTile": { "tx": 5, "ty": 9 },    // for move
 *     "targetCharacter": "character_name",   // for talk
 *     "dialogue": "optional speech",
 *     "targetDialogue": "response from target character", // for talk
 *     "description": "what the character is doing" }
 */

feat: add 'talk' action so characters can converse with each other (FRG-206)

- AIEngine._requestDecision now filters nearby characters (within 8 tiles)
  and includes them in the prompt context (name, label, position, action)
- Added 'talk' as a fourth action choice in the user prompt with
  targetCharacter, dialogue, and targetDialogue fields
- AIEngine._applyDecision handles 'talk': walks Character A to an adjacent
  tile of Character B, sets Character B's pendingSpeech from targetDialogue,
  and queues a conversation event to SimulationEngine.pendingEvents
- Engine.js passes a pushEvent callback in simState so AIEngine can append
  conversation events to pendingEvents for the debug event log

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create --title "feat: add 'talk' action so characters can converse (FRG-206)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Added nearby characters to the AI prompt context (name, label, position, current action) so the LLM knows who is around
- Added `talk` as a fourth action choice in the prompt with `targetCharacter`, `dialogue`, and `targetDialogue` fields
- Implemented the `talk` case in `_applyDecision`: Character A walks adjacent to Character B, Character B gets `pendingSpeech` set from `targetDialogue`, and a conversation event is pushed to `SimulationEngine.pendingEvents` for the debug log

## Test plan
- [ ] Confirm AI prompt now includes `Nearby characters` context line
- [ ] Confirm characters occasionally pick `talk` and walk toward each other
- [ ] Confirm Character B's speech bubble shows the `targetDialogue` response
- [ ] Confirm conversation events appear in the debug event log with both participant labels
- [ ] Confirm existing `move`, `interact`, and `idle` actions are unaffected

Closes FRG-206

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

content.content: https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-phaser/pull/5

input.body:
## Implementation complete — PR #5

### What was done

**`src/AIEngine.js`**
- `_requestDecision` now receives the full `characters` array and filters nearby characters (within 8 tiles, excluding self), then includes them in the prompt context as `{ name, label, tx, ty, action }`
- Added `talk` as a fourth action choice in the user prompt with `targetCharacter`, `dialogue`, and `targetDialogue` fields
- `_applyDecision` now handles `talk`: finds target by `name`, walks Character A to an adjacent walkable tile of Character B, sets Character B's `pendingSpeech` from `targetDialogue`, and pushes a `{ type: 'conversation', speakerLabel, targetLabel, dialogue, response }` event to the sim

**`src/Engine.js`**
- Added `pushEvent: (ev) => this.pendingEvents.push(ev)` to the `simState` object passed to `aiEngine.update()`, so the AI can append conversation events for the debug log

### Key decision
The AI response includes both `dialogue` (Character A's speech) and `targetDialogue` (Character B's scripted response). This keeps one round-trip per conversation and lets the LLM write both sides. Character B's `pendingSpeech` is set immediately when the decision applies — the speech bubble appears when the scene polls it, which naturally gives Character B time to "hear" A arrive.

PR: https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-phaser/pull/5

content.text:
{"id":"8ee40cbb-a2e5-43b1-b8a8-6c94ee3b9b3b","identifier":"FRG-206","title":"Bug: Characters never talk to each other","description":"AIEngine.\_applyDecision only handles move/interact/idle — there is no talk action. Characters never approach each other for conversation.

## What exists

* AIEngine.\_requestDecision builds a prompt with nearby objects, but NOT nearby characters
* The action choices in the prompt are: move, interact, idle
* Character has: currentDialogue, dialogueTimer, pendingSpeech properties
* SimulationEngine already handles pendingSpeech in its update loop

## Implementation

1. In AIEngine.\_requestDecision: add nearby characters to the prompt context (name, position, current action)
2. Add 'talk' as a fourth action choice in the user prompt:
   { "action": "talk", "targetCharacter": "character_name", "dialogue": "...", "description": "..." }
3. In AIEngine.\_applyDecision: add 'talk' case
   * Find target character by name
   * Walk Character A to adjacent tile of Character B (use findAdjacentWalkable)
   * Set Character A's dialogue
   * Set Character B's pendingSpeech to a contextual response (can be from the AI response or a simple acknowledgment)
4. Add conversation events to SimulationEngine.pendingEvents so the debug event log captures them

## Dependencies

* Depends on FRG-218 (AIEngine wired into debug page) to test on debug page
* Can be developed against main page first if needed

## Acceptance

* AI prompt includes nearby characters as context
* Characters occasionally choose 'talk' action targeting another character
* Character A walks to Character B and speaks
* Character B responds with dialogue
* Conversation appears in debug event log with both participants","projectMilestone":{"id":"d98e487a-66f2-4106-86d3-550aac4cb898","name":"M1: Sim engine correct (debug foundation)"},"priority":{"value":1,"name":"Urgent"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-206/bug-characters-never-talk-to-each-other","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-206-bug-characters-never-talk-to-each-other","createdAt":"2026-03-07T01:01:09.188Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-07T04:27:21.356Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":"2026-03-07T04:27:21.336Z","dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Done","labels":[],"attachments":[{"id":"5596d02b-dc22-47ee-ade1-4491d90c813b","title":"feat: add 'talk' action so characters can converse (FRG-206)","subtitle":null,"url":"https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-phaser/pull/5"}],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","assignee":"Chill","assigneeId":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"AI Aquarium","projectId":"0385a8c7-ba5a-43a3-b223-acf4d1ad6d63","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"}

content.text:
## Status
DONE

## What Changed
- **`src/AIEngine.js`** — Added `characters` parameter through the `update` → `_requestDecision` → `_applyDecision` call chain; added nearby characters to the prompt context; added `talk` as a fourth action choice with `targetCharacter`/`dialogue`/`targetDialogue` fields; implemented `talk` case that walks Character A adjacent to Character B, sets Character B's `pendingSpeech`, and queues a conversation event
- **`src/Engine.js`** — Added `pushEvent: (ev) => this.pendingEvents.push(ev)` to the `simState` object so AIEngine can append conversation events to the debug log

## Key Decisions
- The `targetDialogue` field in the AI response carries Character B's scripted reply — one LLM call writes both sides of the conversation, avoiding a second round-trip
- Character B's `pendingSpeech` is set at decision-apply time (not on arrival), so the speech bubble appears naturally via the existing `pendingSpeech` polling in the scene

## PR / Branch
- Branch: `feature/FRG-206-characters-talk-to-each-other`
- PR: https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-phaser/pull/5
